howItWorks.title: How It Works
howItWorks.startNoAccount: You can start in under a minute by checking any service—no account needed.
howItWorks.subtitle: Learn how our AI-powered Privacy-Preserving Personalization creates a unique experience tailored to your specific needs, risks, and technical comfort level

step1.title: Start Your Privacy Journey
step1.description: Begin by taking an assessment to get personalized insights
details: Take an Assessment - Complete one of three educational assessments to get personalized recommendations
Digital Privacy Risk Assessment (10-15 min) - Learn about your digital footprint and identify privacy risks
Privacy Rights Assessment (5-10 min) - Learn about and understand your privacy rights
Security Awareness Assessment (15-20 min) - Educational security evaluation including ransomware awareness

step2.title: Persona Detection & Personalization
step2.description: Your privacy profile is identified through AI analysis of your assessment responses
details: AI analyzes behavioral patterns, risk tolerance, and privacy concerns locally on your device
Persona detection from 9 distinct profiles: Cautious Parent, Digital Novice, Privacy Advocate, Online Shopper, Social Influencer, Private Individual, Concerned Employee, Data Controller, and Student
Your persona determines what you see in your personalized dashboard
All processing happens locally - your data never leaves your device

step3.title: Personalized Privacy Dashboard
step3.description: Access your customized dashboard that adapts to your persona, assessment results, and privacy goals
details: Persona-specific priority actions and recommendations
Customized metrics and progress tracking based on your assessment results
Adaptive resource recommendations filtered by your persona and skill level
Service-based privacy insights and risk analysis
Integrated tools: Privacy Assistant Bot, Interactive Action Planner, and more
Real-time updates as you complete assessments and take actions

step4.title: Enhanced Recommendations & Action Plans
step4.description: Receive comprehensive, actionable guidance with multi-phase learning journeys and personalized action plans
details: Overall risk/awareness strategy dashboard with visual insights
Multi-phase learning journey (Foundation Building, Knowledge Expansion, Mastery)
Category-specific action plans with immediate actions, tools, and follow-up timelines
Behavioral pattern detection and personalized learning recommendations
Essential resource library with curated tools and educational materials
Integration with Privacy Toolkit, Services Monitoring, and Adaptive Resources

roadmap.title: Your Privacy Workflow
roadmap.description: Follow this 3-step workflow to get personalized privacy insights. Start with Services Monitoring, set your privacy concerns (required), and then take the assessment to unlock your personalized dashboard.

step1.title: Browse Services Monitoring
step1.description: Explore our catalog of 200+ services. See privacy exposure indices, risk levels, and detailed privacy profiles for each service you use.
details: Search and filter services by category or name
View privacy exposure indices (0-100 scale)
See risk levels: Low, Medium, High, Critical
Read detailed privacy profiles and data collection practices
Select services to track and monitor
No signup required - start browsing immediately

step2.title: Set Privacy Concerns
step2.description: Select your privacy concerns from 4 focus areas (Family & children, Financial & shopping, Work & professional, General). This is REQUIRED and is the PRIMARY method of personalization.
details: Select at least one privacy concern (REQUIRED)
Choose from 4 focus areas: Family & children, Financial & shopping, Work & professional, General
Your concerns drive personalization
Your concerns determine what you see in your personalized dashboard
All processing happens locally - your data never leaves your device

step3.title: Take Privacy Assessment
step3.description: Complete our privacy assessment to get comprehensive insights, access your personalized dashboard, and receive actionable recommendations.
details: Digital Privacy Risk Assessment (10-15 min) - Learn about your digital footprint
Privacy Rights Assessment (5-10 min) - Understand your privacy rights
Security Awareness Assessment (15-20 min) - Educational security evaluation
Complete Assessment - Comprehensive evaluation combining all three
Get personalized dashboard with actionable insights
Access advanced analytics and historical tracking (Standard plan)

outcomes: Risk level assessment by behavior category
exposure.businessValue: Identifies immediate privacy risks that could lead to data breaches, identity theft, or unauthorized surveillance

rights.businessValue: Empowers you to effectively exercise your legal privacy rights and take control of your personal data
coverageAreas: GDPR Article 13-14 - Right to be Informed
GDPR Article 15 - Right to Access Personal Data
GDPR Article 17 - Right to Erasure (Right to be Forgotten)
GDPR Article 18 - Right to Restrict Processing
GDPR Article 20 - Right to Data Portability
GDPR Article 21 - Right to Object to Processing
CCPA Consumer Rights and Enforcement Mechanisms

full.businessValue: Provides a comprehensive, personalized action plan based on your responses to real-world scenarios and advanced privacy topics

completeAssessment.title: Complete Privacy Assessment
completeAssessment.subtitle: Scenario-based evaluation with personalized action plan generation

ui.thisWillBeIncluded: This will be included in your action plan:

phishingScenario.question: You receive an email from your bank asking you to verify your account. The email looks official and includes your bank's logo. It asks you to click a link and provide your Social Security Number, date of birth, and mother's maiden name. What do you do?
phishingScenario.explanation: This scenario tests your ability to identify phishing attempts and protect sensitive information. Legitimate banks never ask for sensitive information via email.

clickAndProvide.label: Click the link and provide the information - it looks official

clickButVerify.label: Click the link but verify the website looks legitimate before entering info
actionItems: Use official apps instead of email links

verifyFirst.label: Contact the bank directly using their official phone number to verify the request

ignoreAndReport.label: Ignore the email and report it as phishing to the bank and authorities

appPermissionScenario.question: A new fitness app you want to use requests access to your contacts, location (always), camera, microphone, and photo library. The privacy policy is 50 pages long. How do you proceed?
appPermissionScenario.explanation: This scenario tests your understanding of data minimization and consent principles. Apps often request more permissions than necessary.

acceptAll.label: Accept all permissions - the app needs them to work properly

readPolicy.label: Read the full privacy policy first, then decide
actionItems: Learn to identify key privacy policy sections
Consider alternative apps with better privacy

selectivePermissions.label: Grant only essential permissions (location when using app, camera for photos)

declineAndFindAlternative.label: Decline and look for a privacy-focused alternative that requests minimal data

dataBreachScenario.question: You receive a notification that a company you have an account with experienced a data breach. Your email, password hash, and purchase history were exposed. What are your immediate actions?
dataBreachScenario.explanation: This scenario tests your knowledge of breach response and privacy rights, including your right to be informed and take protective action.

ignore.label: Ignore it - companies handle these things

changePassword.label: Change the password for that account only
actionItems: Use unique passwords for all accounts

comprehensiveResponse.label: Change password, enable 2FA, check for password reuse, monitor accounts, and request data deletion if possible

fullRightsExercise.label: All of the above, plus file a complaint with regulators, request full data copy, and exercise deletion rights
actionItems: Help others understand their breach rights

socialMediaPrivacyScenario.question: You discover that a social media platform changed its privacy settings and your posts are now public by default, exposing personal information you thought was private. What do you do?
socialMediaPrivacyScenario.explanation: This scenario tests your awareness of privacy settings management and your rights to control data sharing.

acceptChange.label: Accept the change - it's the platform's right to update settings

adjustSettings.label: Quickly adjust your privacy settings back to private

comprehensiveAudit.label: Audit all privacy settings, review past posts, delete sensitive content, and set up change notifications

exerciseRights.label: All of the above, plus file a complaint about the change, request data deletion, and consider switching platforms

aiPrivacyAwareness.question: How concerned are you about AI systems using your personal data for training, profiling, or automated decision-making?
aiPrivacyAwareness.explanation: AI and machine learning raise new privacy concerns including algorithmic bias, automated decision-making, and data scraping for training models.

notConcerned.label: Not concerned - AI is helpful and I trust companies to use my data responsibly

somewhatConcerned.label: Somewhat concerned, but not sure what I can do about it
actionItems: Opt-out of AI training where possible

veryConcerned.label: Very concerned - I actively avoid AI services that use my data for training

activelyManaging.label: Actively managing - I opt-out of AI training, request human review of automated decisions, and use privacy-first AI tools

biometricDataAwareness.question: How do you manage biometric data (fingerprints, face scans, voice prints) collected by devices and services?
biometricDataAwareness.explanation: Biometric data is considered highly sensitive under privacy laws and requires special protection. Once compromised, biometrics cannot be changed like passwords.

notAware.label: I'm not aware that services collect or use my biometric data

acceptAll.label: I accept biometric authentication when offered - it's convenient

selectiveUse.label: I use biometrics selectively - only for device unlock, not for sensitive accounts

minimizeBiometrics.label: I minimize biometric data collection and prefer password/PIN alternatives for sensitive services

crossBorderDataTransfers.question: How aware are you of where your personal data is stored and processed when you use online services?
crossBorderDataTransfers.explanation: Cross-border data transfers are regulated under GDPR and other laws. Data stored in different countries may have different privacy protections.

notAware.label: I don't know or care where my data is stored - it's the company's responsibility

somewhatAware.label: I know some services store data in different countries, but I'm not sure of the implications
actionItems: Prefer services with local data storage

awareAndSelective.label: I prefer services that store data in my country/region and check data location policies
actionItems: Continue selecting services by data location

activelyManaging.label: I actively choose services based on data location, understand transfer mechanisms, and exercise my rights regarding cross-border transfers

privacyByDesignAwareness.question: When choosing new services or products, how much do you consider their privacy-by-design approach?
privacyByDesignAwareness.explanation: Privacy by Design means privacy is built into products from the start, not added as an afterthought. This is a key principle in modern privacy law.

notConsidered.label: I don't consider privacy architecture - I choose based on features and price
actionItems: Research privacy practices before choosing services
Consider privacy as a key feature

sometimesConsidered.label: I sometimes check privacy policies, but features are more important
actionItems: Make privacy a priority in decisions

oftenConsidered.label: I often research privacy practices and prefer companies with strong privacy commitments

primaryFactor.label: Privacy architecture is a primary factor - I actively seek and support privacy-by-design products

privacyGoals.question: What is your primary privacy goal right now?
privacyGoals.explanation: Understanding your goals helps us create a personalized action plan with the right priorities for you.

reduceRisk.label: Reduce my privacy risk exposure and protect against identity theft

learnRights.label: Learn and exercise my privacy rights effectively
actionItems: Learn about GDPR and CCPA rights

minimizeFootprint.label: Minimize my digital footprint and data collection

comprehensive.label: Comprehensive privacy improvement across all areas

timeCommitment.question: How much time can you commit to improving your privacy each week?
timeCommitment.explanation: Realistic time commitments help create achievable action plans that you can actually complete.

minimal.label: Minimal (15-30 minutes) - Quick wins and essential improvements only

moderate.label: Moderate (1-2 hours) - Regular improvements and learning
actionItems: Mix of quick wins and learning

substantial.label: Substantial (3-5 hours) - Comprehensive privacy overhaul
actionItems: Complete account migration to privacy tools

extensive.label: Extensive (5+ hours) - Privacy as a lifestyle priority

biggestConcern.question: What is your biggest privacy concern right now?
biggestConcern.explanation: Identifying your primary concern helps prioritize the most relevant actions in your personalized plan.

dashboard.whatYouCanDo: Here you can see your risk score, selected services, and next steps.
dashboard.leRiskScoreOverall: Overall Law Enforcement risk score (lower = safer)

underutilizedRights.description: You're not fully exercising your privacy rights. Learn how to request data deletion and exercise your rights.

tips.priorityActions: Focus on the red "high priority" actions first - they give you the biggest privacy boost.

howToUse.title: How to Use Services Monitoring
howToUse.description: Our services monitoring provides transparent, regulation-based privacy assessments for online services. Each service is evaluated using a comprehensive methodology that considers privacy risks, regulatory compliance (GDPR, CCPA, CPRA, COPPA, PIPEDA, LGPD, HIPAA, FERPA, GLBA, VCDPA), data collection practices, breach history, and user control mechanisms. Follow these simple steps to discover and monitor privacy risks—everything updates instantly as you interact with the services.
howToUse.methodologyNote: Privacy assessments are based on publicly available information, privacy policies, regulatory filings, and established privacy frameworks including GDPR Article 13 (transparency), CCPA Section 1798.100 (right to know), PIPEDA Principle 4 (limiting collection), LGPD Article 18 (data subject rights), HIPAA privacy and security rules, FERPA student privacy protections, GLBA financial privacy requirements, and VCDPA consumer data rights.

personalizationPrompt.title: Unlock Personalized Content & Insights

needToPay.question: Do I need to pay to use SocialCaution?
needToPay.answer: No! SocialCaution has a Basic (Free) plan that includes everything you need to get started. You can browse 200+ services, take 3 privacy assessments per month, see privacy risk scores, use privacy tools, and export 1 PDF report per month - all for free. All your data stays on your device. The Standard ($2.99/month) plan removes the limits and adds detailed analysis, unlimited assessments, unlimited exports, and email support.

difference.question: What's the difference between Basic and Standard plans?
difference.answer: Basic (Free): Everything you need to start protecting your privacy. Includes 3 assessments per month, track up to 5 services, privacy risk scores with a concise summary, privacy tools, and 1 PDF export per month. All free, all stored on your device. Standard ($2.99/month): Everything in Basic, plus unlimited assessments, full detailed analysis and score breakdown, unlimited service tracking, unlimited exports (PDF/Excel/CSV/JSON), privacy history tracking, data export option, and email support. All for $2.99/month.

cancel.question: Can I cancel anytime?
cancel.answer: Yes! You can cancel your subscription anytime. Your data will stay on your device, and you'll still have access to all the free features.

support.question: How does email support work?
support.answer: If you have questions or need help, just send us an email. We'll do our best to respond quickly, especially for security issues. There's no dedicated support team - it's just us responding to emails, so response times may vary.

catalog.question: How is the service catalog updated?
catalog.answer: Our service catalog includes privacy information for 200+ services. We regularly update it with the latest privacy information we can find from public sources like privacy policies, news reports, and data breach databases.

platformNote.title: Important: Platform-Specific Subscription

platformNote.noSync: Your data stays local and private—but subscriptions do not sync across devices
platformNote.recommendation: Subscribe on the device you use most frequently for privacy management.

exposureIndexMethodology.educationalDisclaimer: Educational Platform: This methodology is designed for educational purposes to help you understand privacy risks. It is not a professional security audit or legal compliance assessment.
exposureIndexMethodology.lawEnforcementIntro: A separate score (0–100) for how a service behaves when government agencies request user data. Lower score = lower risk (the service puts more procedural obstacles in the way). It is shown when you have "Law Enforcement & Government Access" in your Privacy Focus.
exposureIndexMethodology.lawEnforcementHowBuilt: Risk points are added for weaker practices; minimal data retention can reduce risk.
exposureIndexMethodology.lawEnforcementWarrant: Warrant requirement (up to 45 points risk): No warrant required adds the most risk; requiring a court-issued warrant before disclosing data adds none (warrants require judicial approval, unlike many administrative subpoenas). If only metadata is covered by a warrant, part of the points apply.
exposureIndexMethodology.lawEnforcementNotify: User notification (up to 33 points risk): Notifying users before complying with a request (so they can contest) adds no risk; not notifying adds risk.
exposureIndexMethodology.lawEnforcementTransparency: Transparency reporting (up to 22 points risk): Publishing aggregate data on government requests (e.g. transparency report) adds no risk; not doing so adds risk, with partial credit if the service publicizes request volume but does not publish a full report.
exposureIndexMethodology.lawEnforcementRetention: Minimal retention (bonus): Services that retain little or no data for law-enforcement purposes get a small risk reduction (up to 5 points), since there is less to hand over.
exposureIndexMethodology.lawEnforcementDisclaimer: A low score does not mean a service is immune to government access—it means the service puts meaningful procedural obstacles in the way. A high score means it does not. Regulatory compliance (e.g. GDPR) is not part of this score; it is covered in the main Privacy Exposure Index.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about personalized privacy protection
faq.seoDescription: Frequently asked questions about SocialCaution's AI-powered privacy assessment, persona detection, and personalized recommendations.
faq.seoKeywords: privacy platform FAQ, persona detection questions, privacy assessment help, SocialCaution support
faq.contactDescription: Our privacy experts are here to help you protect your digital life

gettingStarted.personaQuestion: What is a privacy persona and why does it matter?
gettingStarted.personaAnswer: A privacy persona is your unique privacy personality profile based on your concerns, technical comfort level, and protection needs. We identify 9 distinct personas: Cautious Parent, Digital Novice, Privacy Advocate, Online Shopper, Social Influencer, Private Individual, Concerned Employee, Data Controller, and Student. Knowing your persona helps us provide relevant recommendations instead of generic advice that doesn't fit your situation.
gettingStarted.assessmentTimeQuestion: How long does the assessment take?
gettingStarted.assessmentTimeAnswer: We offer three assessment options: Privacy Risk Exposure Assessment (5-7 minutes), Privacy Rights Knowledge Checkup (8-12 minutes), and Complete Privacy Assessment (13-19 minutes). You can start with any assessment based on your available time and current privacy focus.
gettingStarted.accountQuestion: Do I need to create an account?
gettingStarted.accountAnswer: No! SocialCaution works completely without registration on the free plan. You can take assessments, get personalized recommendations, and access your dashboard anonymously. All your data stays in your browser's local storage under your complete control. Premium and Family plans offer optional encrypted data export for manual backup to your own storage.
gettingStarted.freePlanAnswer: Yes! SocialCaution offers a free plan with limited features. The free plan includes: 3 privacy assessments per month (limit resets monthly), Track up to 5 services maximum, 1 PDF export per month, All data stored locally on your device, No account required. IMPORTANT: The free plan has limits. For unlimited assessments, unlimited service tracking, unlimited exports (PDF/Excel/JSON), optional encrypted data export for manual backup, and advanced features, you need a paid plan: Premium ($2.99/month) or Family ($7.99/month). You can use the free plan indefinitely, but will need to upgrade if you exceed the monthly limits.

pricingPlans.freePlanQuestion: What's included in the Free plan?
pricingPlans.freePlanAnswer: The Free plan includes essential privacy features with specific limits: Privacy assessments (limited to 3 per month), AI-powered persona detection, Privacy Exposure Index (unified 8-factor scoring with concise summary), service catalog access (browse 200+, track up to 5), personalized dashboard, adaptive resources, and privacy tools directory. Free plan limits: Track up to 5 services maximum, 1 PDF export per month, No Excel or JSON exports, All data stored locally (privacy-first approach), No advanced analytics. All features work without an account - your data stays on your device.
pricingPlans.planDifferenceQuestion: What's the difference between Free, Premium, and Family plans?
pricingPlans.planDifferenceAnswer: FREE PLAN (No payment required): Limited to 3 privacy assessments per month, Track up to 5 services maximum, 1 PDF export per month, No Excel or JSON exports, Local storage only (privacy-first approach), Basic analytics only. PREMIUM PLAN ($2.99/month or $24.99/year): Everything in Free, plus: Unlimited privacy assessments, Unlimited service tracking, Unlimited PDF/Excel/CSV/JSON exports, Optional encrypted data export for manual backup, Privacy analytics dashboard with assessment history, View assessment history and score changes, Privacy guides and curated external tools directory, Email support with faster response for security issues, Service catalog and risk assessments managed by cybersecurity and legal professionals, Early access to new features. FAMILY PLAN ($7.99/month or $64.99/year): Everything in Premium, plus: Up to 5 family members, Family privacy dashboard, Child protection features, Family privacy reports, Shared privacy goals, Family privacy coaching.
pricingPlans.cancelQuestion: Can I cancel my subscription anytime?
pricingPlans.cancelAnswer: Yes! You can cancel your Premium or Family subscription at any time. Your data will remain accessible, and you'll continue to have access to all free plan features. There are no cancellation fees or penalties.
pricingPlans.oneTimePurchaseQuestion: What are the one-time purchase products?
pricingPlans.oneTimePurchaseAnswer: In addition to subscription plans, we offer one-time purchase products (these require payment): Privacy Assessment Toolkit ($49 one-time) - Advanced offline privacy tools with lifetime access; Service Monitoring Alerts ($29 one-time) - Lifetime monitoring and alerts for privacy policy changes; Premium Report Templates ($19 one-time) - Professional PDF report templates with customization; Professional Privacy Audit ($299 one-time) - 1-on-1 consultation with privacy experts including comprehensive audit report. All one-time purchases provide lifetime access with no recurring fees. These are separate from the free plan and subscription plans.
pricingPlans.annualBillingQuestion: Do you offer annual billing discounts?
pricingPlans.annualBillingAnswer: Yes! Premium plan: $24.99/year (save 30% vs monthly, equivalent to $2.08/month). Family plan: $64.99/year (save 32% vs monthly, equivalent to $5.41/month). Annual billing saves you money and provides the same unlimited features as monthly billing.
pricingPlans.paymentRequiredQuestion: What requires payment and what is free?
pricingPlans.paymentRequiredAnswer: FREE (No payment required): Basic privacy assessments (3 per month), Service catalog access, Persona detection, Privacy Exposure Index, Basic dashboard, Track up to 5 services, 1 PDF export per month, All data stored locally. REQUIRES PAYMENT: Premium subscription ($2.99/month) - Unlimited assessments, unlimited services, unlimited exports, optional encrypted data export for manual backup, advanced analytics. Family subscription ($7.99/month) - Everything in Premium plus family features. One-time purchases: Privacy Toolkit ($49), Service Monitoring ($29), Report Templates ($19), Professional Audit ($299).

privacySecurity.protectPrivacyQuestion: How do you protect my privacy while providing personalization?
privacySecurity.protectPrivacyAnswer: Everything happens locally in your browser. Our AI algorithms run client-side to analyze your responses and detect your persona without sending any personal data to our servers. We use anonymous analytics to improve the platform, but no assessment answers or personal information is ever transmitted or stored by us.
privacySecurity.dataCollectionQuestion: What data do you actually collect?
privacySecurity.dataCollectionAnswer: We collect zero personal information. Our anonymous analytics track only: page views (no personal content), assessment completion rates (not your actual answers), feature usage patterns (aggregated), and technical performance metrics. We cannot trace any of this back to individual users.
privacySecurity.dataPrivateQuestion: How do I know my data stays private?
privacySecurity.dataPrivateAnswer: You can verify our privacy claims by: inspecting network traffic (you'll see no personal data transmitted), checking your browser's local storage (all data stays local), using browser developer tools to audit our practices, and reviewing our open-source code (coming soon).
privacySecurity.gdprCompliantAnswer: Yes, but in the best way possible - by not collecting personal data at all. Since we don't process personal information, most privacy law obligations don't apply to us. This means you get the strongest possible privacy protection without needing to exercise rights or file requests.

howItWorks.personaAccuracyQuestion: How accurate is the persona detection?
howItWorks.personalizedAnswer: Recommendations are tailored based on: your privacy concerns, current risk assessment scores, technical comfort level indicated in responses, specific vulnerabilities identified in your assessment, and priority concerns you've selected.
howItWorks.retakeQuestion: How often should I retake assessments?
howItWorks.retakeAnswer: We recommend retaking assessments every 3-6 months or after making significant privacy improvements. This helps track your progress, updates your risk scores, and ensures your recommendations stay current with your evolving privacy practices. NOTE: Free plan users are limited to 3 assessments per month, so plan accordingly. Premium and Family plans have unlimited assessments.

technicalQuestions.technologyQuestion: What technology powers the AI persona detection?
technicalQuestions.technologyAnswer: Our persona detection uses client-side machine learning algorithms that run entirely in your browser. The system analyzes response patterns, risk indicators, and behavioral preferences using weighted scoring models and pattern recognition - all without sending your data anywhere.
technicalQuestions.offlineAnswer: The core assessment and persona detection work offline once the page loads. However, accessing external resources, tools, and updated content requires an internet connection. Your assessment results are always available offline since they're stored locally.
technicalQuestions.browsersAnswer: SocialCaution works on all modern browsers including Chrome, Firefox, Safari, and Edge. We use standard web technologies and don't require any special plugins or extensions. For the best experience, we recommend using an up-to-date browser version.
technicalQuestions.exportQuestion: Can I export my assessment results?
technicalQuestions.exportAnswer: Yes, but export options vary by plan. FREE PLAN: 1 PDF export per month (no Excel or JSON exports). PREMIUM & FAMILY PLANS: Unlimited PDF, Excel, and JSON exports. All exports are generated locally on your device. Premium and Family subscribers can also export their full assessment history and analytics data.
technicalQuestions.limitsQuestion: What happens when I reach my free plan limits?
technicalQuestions.limitsAnswer: When you reach your free plan limits (3 assessments per month, 5 services tracked, or 1 PDF export per month), you'll see a notification explaining the limit. You can either wait until the next month for limits to reset, or upgrade to Premium ($2.99/month) or Family ($7.99/month) for unlimited access to all features. Your existing data remains accessible regardless of your plan.
